title: Senior Architectural Coordinator (HDB Projects)
topic_label: contractors design requirements, architectural designs, architectural design
description:
Job
Summary The
Architectural Coordinator is responsible for coordinating and managing all
architectural works for HDB projects, ensuring compliance with approved
drawings, HDB specifications, quality standards, and project timelines. The
role focuses on drawing coordination, site interface management, quality
control, and close liaison with HDB, consultants, and site teams. Key
Responsibilities 1.
Architectural Coordination & Interface Management Coordinate architectural works with structural and M&E
     trades to ensure proper interfaces and sequencing. Resolve architectural clashes and site issues related to finishes,
     openings, details, and tolerances. Ensure architectural works integrate smoothly with M&E
     services, precast elements, and structural components. 2.
Drawings & Technical Submissions Review and coordinate architectural drawings, shop drawings, and
     builder’s work drawings . Prepare and submit material submissions, mock-ups, samples, and
     method statements for approval. Raise and manage RFI / technical queries with consultants
     and HDB. 3. Site
Supervision & Quality Control Conduct regular site inspections for architectural works including: Blockwork, plastering, screeding, and tiling Waterproofing works Doors, windows, ironmongery, and glazing Painting, ceilings, and partitions Ensure works comply with HDB specifications, approved details,
     and CONQUAS standards . Coordinate and close out architectural-related NCRs. 4.
Programme & Progress Coordination Coordinate architectural work sequence in line with overall project
     schedule. Monitor progress and highlight delays or quality risks to the
     Project Manager. Coordinate manpower deployment and material delivery with
     subcontractors. 5. HDB,
Consultant & Authority Liaison Liaise with HDB, architects, and consultants on
     architectural matters. Attend site meetings, coordination meetings, and architectural
     inspections. Ensure timely implementation of instructions and comments from HDB
     and consultants. 6.
Mock-ups, Inspections & Handover Coordinate mock-up units, sample panels, and workmanship
     demonstrations . Prepare for and attend HDB / consultant inspections and CONQUAS
     assessments . Assist in preparation of as-built drawings and handover
     documentation . 7.
Defects Management Coordinate defects rectification during TOP / CSC inspections
     and Defects Liability Period (DLP) . Ensure architectural defects are rectified to HDB and consultant
     satisfaction. Requirements
& Qualifications Diploma or Degree in Architecture, Building, or Construction
     Management . Minimum 3–5 years of relevant experience in HDB building
     projects . Strong knowledge of HDB specifications, construction details,
     and CONQUAS requirements . Familiar with precast and PPVC architectural interfaces
     (preferred). Good coordination, communication, and documentation skills. Key
Skills Architectural drawing coordination HDB quality & CONQUAS control Site coordination & inspection Technical submissions & RFIs Defects management What we offer We are committed to providing our employees with a supportive and rewarding work environment. In addition to a competitive salary, we offer a range of benefits, including: Competitive salary and performance-based bonuses. Opportunities for professional development and career advancement. A collaborative and team-oriented culture that values innovation and continuous improvement. Apply now to become our next Senior Architectural Coordinator and contribute to the success of our projects.

title: Senior Researcher/ Researcher (Botanical Research – Herbarium)
topic_label: plantation gardener, gardener, landscaping
description: What the role is The Singapore Botanic Gardens (SBG) is one of the world's oldest tropical botanical gardens and one of only three botanic gardens globally recognised as a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The institution undertakes a range of research including taxonomy and systematics (including phylogenomics), conservation, forest and tree ecology, as well as other programmes in horticulture and urban ecology. The Botanical Research Branch conducts taxonomy and systematics research on Malesian flora across the Herbarium, Molecular and Micropropagation Laboratories, and Seed Bank, with particular focus on Singapore's native species and regional plant diversity alongside conservation work on key groups including Orchidaceae and Zingiberaceae. The Singapore Herbarium (SING) holds approximately 800,000 collections from Singapore and the region, with extensive holdings from Peninsular Malaysia, Borneo, and New Guinea, and is currently undergoing digitalisation. The Gardens publishes the peer-reviewed journal Gardens' Bulletin Singapore and is overseeing the writing and publication of the Flora of Singapore in parallel with a genomic flora of Singapore's native species. This role will be primarily based in SING and undertaking research into the plant diversity of Singapore and Malesia. What you will be working on You will be a member of the team working on research on the plant (angiosperm) diversity of Singapore and surrounding regions. We are seeking candidates with demonstrated experience and research expertise of collections-based research on plant taxonomy and systematics. The candidate’s research background should include applications of systematic theory and techniques, biogeography, understanding of morphological descriptive taxonomy and have working experience of employing modern tools for collections-based research including machine learning and/or biogeographic analysis. The candidate will be expected to develop a research programme on angiosperm groups for which Singapore Botanic Gardens has considerable holdings (e.g., Gentianales, Poales, Arecaceae), but candidates with research in other plant groups with high diversity in Southeast Asia will be considered. Opportunities for collaboration with regional partners and fieldwork will be possible and encouraged where they complement SBG's research aims. In addition, the job holder is expected to leverage modern computational tools to develop research questions and publish research outcomes from the digital dataset generated as part of the SING digitalisation programme. Specifically, you will: - Initiate and drive exciting research projects to understand the diversity, evolution, biogeography and classification of important tropical groups found in Singapore and surrounding regions. - Curate and enhance the holdings in SING through fieldwork and the acquisition of specimens, as well as critical identifications following new findings. - Make use of rich image datasets and develop innovative research questions and projects with both internal and external stakeholders and collaborators employing new computational methods. - Analyse data, publish findings in reputable peer-reviewed journals and share findings at scientific conferences. - Communicate and share knowledge on plant diversity, herbarium research and conservation with students and non-scientist stakeholders, through giving talks and lectures, mentoring students, publishing popular scientific articles, media interviews etc. - Support science across Singapore Botanic Gardens and within the National Parks Board, including involvement in projects on other research programmes, involvement in workshops and working groups, etc. What we are looking for - Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) in botany or related discipline with a taxonomic focus on angiosperms. - Experience with herbarium-based research, proficiency in the statistical programming language R, Python and other statistical programming languages, evidence of working knowledge of innovative technologies to leverage data generated from specimens (e.g., ML from digital images, occurrence data etc.). - Keen interest in the plant diversity of tropical Asia, integrative taxonomy, and a knowledge of contemporary APG classification (preferably with experience in species-rich tropical groups in Singapore and Malesia). - Ability to formulate independent research projects with experience in scientific publishing in reputable scientific journals and drafting research proposals. - Able to conduct field work for plant collections, including overseas travel. - Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with strong interpersonal abilities and capacity to work both independently and collaboratively in a team environment under time pressure. - Self-motivated, organised, and open-minded, with willingness to take on roles and responsibilities to support the organisation’s functions and mission. Only shortlisted candidates are notified.

title: Research Fellow (Biological Sciences)
topic_label: plantation gardener, gardener, landscaping
description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS
Career Portal Your application will be processed only if you apply
via NUS
Career Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description The Plant Ecology, Evolution and Biogeography (PEEB) Lab has two Research Fellow positions on a project entitled “A global modelling framework for frugivory and seed dispersal interactions to understand ecosystem functioning in the human modified tropics”.  Appointment is for an initial period of one year with the possibility of renewal for an additional year. Successful candidates will be expected to: (Co-)develop, implement and lead impactful research including but not limited to the following topics: Modelling the impacts of defaunation / refaunation on seed dispersal functioning globally Developing new methodologies for the modelling of seed dispersal interactions Understanding how frugivory and seed dispersal interactions are structured across the tropics Mentor graduate and undergraduate students Qualifications Ph.D. (or have a Ph.D. conferred within 6 months of the appointment commencing) in Macroecology, Seed Dispersal Ecology, Ecological Modelling, or related field. Application procedure: Applicants should submit: cover letter, research statement explaining the applicant’s research experience and aptitude in relation to job description, curriculum vitae with publication record, and contact information for three referees. Applications will be evaluated on a rolling basis until the positions are filled. Interested applicants may get in touch with Jun Ying Lim ( jylim@nus.edu.sg ) with any questions.

title: Pest control technician
topic_label: plantation gardener, gardener, landscaping
description:
Hey bros! LOOKING FOR EXPERIENCED 3YEARS AND ABOVE PEST CONTROL TECH. 



+Drive home van

+Flexible hours



Key Responsibilities (Job Scope):



· Conduct thorough site inspections and assessments to identify pest infestations (e.g., rodents, termites, cockroaches, mosquitoes, bed bugs) and conducive conditions.

· Develop, propose, and execute targeted Integrated Pest Management (IPM) treatment plans using a variety of methods (chemical, non-chemical, baiting, trapping, exclusion).

· Safely handle, apply, and store pesticides and chemicals in strict compliance with Singapore's National Environment Agency (NEA) guidelines and Environmental Public Health (EPH) Act.

· Prepare detailed and accurate service reports, treatment records, recommendations, and certificates using digital or mobile systems.

· Provide expert advice to clients on pest prevention measures and follow-up schedules.

· Maintain, calibrate, and ensure the good condition of all pest control equipment and tools.

· Respond promptly to urgent service calls and customer feedback.

· Assist in training and guiding junior technicians on best practices and safety protocols.

· Uphold the highest standards of professionalism, customer service, and safety at all client sites.



Requirements:



· Mandatory: Valid Singapore Pest Control Technician Licence issued by the National Environment Agency (NEA).

· Minimum of 3 years of hands-on field experience in pest control within Singapore.

· Strong knowledge of local pest species, their biology, and behavior.

· In-depth understanding of NEA regulations, SAFE management procedures, and the use of approved pesticides.

· Proven ability to diagnose complex pest issues and implement effective solutions.

· Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to explain technical details to clients clearly.

· Physically fit to handle equipment, work in various environments, and sometimes in confined spaces.

· Possess a Class 3 driving license is a strong advantage.

· Responsible, punctual, honest, and with a strong service mindset.

title: Senior Project Manager – Procurement & FF&E (Interior Design)
topic_label: interior design consultant, senior interior designer, interior design firm
description:
Role Overview The Senior Project Manager – Procurement & FF&E is responsible for the strategic planning and hands-on execution of procurement , alongside the end-to-end delivery of multiple interior design projects across hospitality, commercial, and high-end residential sectors . The role spans all interior design disciplines and manages projects for both B2B clients (developers, operators, corporate clients) and B2C clients (private residential clients), across Singapore, the UAE, and Australia . This position holds full accountability for procurement strategy —including sourcing approach, supplier and manufacturer selection, budgeting, lead-time planning, logistics planning, and risk mitigation—as well as the physical placement and management of orders , from issuance of purchase orders through to delivery, installation, and final reconciliation. Working closely with the Creative Director, General Manager, and Senior Design Team , and in daily coordination with the Finance team , the role ensures that procurement and project decisions align with design intent, programme, cash flow, and overall commercial performance . Key Responsibilities 1. Project Management & Turnkey Delivery Manage multiple interior design projects concurrently across hospitality, commercial, and residential sectors Work in close alignment with Lead Designers to support and facilitate delivery from early coordination and feasibility , through design development, procurement, construction, installation, and final handover Develop and maintain project programmes, milestones, budgets, and cash flow schedules Support and coordinate internal design teams , external consultants (architects, MEP, QS), contractors, and suppliers Lead and attend site meetings as required, monitor site progress, and ensure delivery aligns with approved drawings, specifications, and design intent Identify and manage project risks, scope changes, and variations , ensuring clear documentation, approvals, and early escalation of variations, out-of-scope works, or cost impacts 2. Procurement Strategy (Planning &
Control) Develop and lead, in collaboration with the Senior Design Team , the procurement strategy for each project, including: Sourcing approach (local vs international) Supplier and manufacturer selection Lead-time planning and procurement phasing Logistics, warehousing, and installation sequencing Risk assessment and mitigation strategies Prepare and manage procurement schedules, specifications, budgets, and cash flow forecasts , aligned with the project programme Advise the Creative Director and General Manager on procurement decisions that impact design intent, programme, and financial exposure Present procurement strategies, recommendations, and approvals clearly and professionally to B2B and B2C clients 3. Procurement Execution & Order Placement Take direct responsibility for the placement of all FF&E and related procurement orders , including: Issuing purchase orders Managing supplier confirmations and documentation Tracking production, delivery, and installation timelines Lead price negotiations, payment terms, and delivery conditions with suppliers and manufacturers Ensure all orders are placed strictly in line with approved budgets, scopes, cash flow plans, and internal approvals Manage substitutions, re-selections, and supply chain changes as required, while protecting design intent and quality 4. Financial & Commercial Management Work closely with the Finance team to: Track procurement commitments and expenditure Manage deposits, staged payments, and final balances Support accurate forecasting and invoicing Monitor project margins, procurement exposure, and cash flow risks Ensure all financial controls, approval protocols, and internal governance requirements are followed Ensure SOPs are strictly adhered to for order placement, including verification that deposits and client payments have been received prior to placing orders, to ensure ALBD is not financially exposed or out of pocket 5. Supply Chain, Logistics & Installation Oversee production timelines, quality control, shipping, customs clearance, warehousing, and delivery Coordinate on-site delivery, installation, styling, snagging, and defects rectification Ensure all FF&E and finishes are installed in accordance with approved specifications, drawings, and design intent close-out documentation, warranties, and final financial and procurement reconciliations 6. Client & Stakeholder Management Act as a senior client-facing lead , providing confident, structured, and solutions-oriented communication Manage expectations across B2B and B2C clients , balancing the design ambition of the Design Lead with programme, budget, and delivery realities Support the Creative Director and General Manager in client presentations, procurement approvals, and progress reporting , ensuring all information is client-ready, accurate, and aligned to enableeffective expectation management Build and maintain trusted relationships with clients, consultants, suppliers, and contractors 7. Quality, Process & Team Collaboration Uphold high standards of design integrity, workmanship, and finish quality Contribute to the refinement and improvement of internal project management, procurement systems, and SOPs Work collaboratively across design, operations, and finance to ensure aligned, efficient delivery Required Experience & Qualifications Minimum 8 years’ experience in interior design–led project management and FF&E procurement Proven experience delivering turnkey interior projects from ground-up to handover Demonstrated ownership of both procurement strategy and order placement Experience across hospitality, commercial, and residential interiors Strong understanding of construction sequencing, FF&E, and site coordination International sourcing and logistics experience across UAE, Australia, and China is highly advantageous Singaporean / Permanent Resident / Work Permit Skills & Competencies Strong project, procurement, and financial management capability Commercially astute with excellent negotiation skills Highly organised, detail-driven, meticulous , and solutions-focused Confident communicator across senior stakeholders and clients Design-literate with a deep respect for creative intent and execution quality Personal Attributes Calm under pressure and comfortable managing complexity Proactive, accountable, and able to operate autonomously Collaborative leader with strong professional presence Adaptable across cultures, geographies, and client types Mandarin-speaking preferred Comfortable working with industry-standard software (e.g. project management, procurement tracking, and financial systems)

With our own in-house carpentry workshop and curated showroom, we take a hands-on approach to creating spaces that reflect the signature identity, lifestyle, and sensibility of each homeowner — thoughtfully resolved within the architectural realities of the home. Our work sits at the intersection of design, buildability, and human experience . We believe in doing things properly — with care, discipline, and respect for the craft. title: Design Director / Senior Architectural Manager (Head of Design)
topic_label: singapore board architects, supervise architectural works, supervise architectural
description:
Design Director / Senior Architectural Manager (Head of
Design) About the Company: We are a fast-growing,
Singapore-based Developer and Builder specializing in advanced modular
construction (PPVC/MiC). With a robust portfolio of residential, hotel, and
serviced apartment projects across Australia and New Zealand, we integrate in-house
design with our dedicated supply chain in China to deliver high-quality
developments. The Role: We are seeking a hands-on, results-driven, and technically authoritative leader to take full
command of our in-house Architectural Design and Drafting team. You will bridge
the gap between our Singapore HQ, Australian/NZ project sites, and Chinese
manufacturing partners. Key Responsibilities: Team Leadership & Performance Management: Lead,mentor, and discipline the architectural team. Transform the current team culture into a high-performance, deadline-oriented unit. Implement strict SOPs, Timesheets, and KPI tracking to ensure maximum productivity and accountability. Review work output rigorously. You are expected to be the "gatekeeper"of quality and efficiency. Technical Compliance (AU/NZ): Oversee all design stages from concept to construction documentation, ensuring strict compliance with BCA (Building Code of Australia), NCC, and NZS standards . Manage the submission process and liaise with Australian/NZ consultants to secure Building Consents/Approvals without delays. DfMA & Supply Chain Integration: Translate architectural designs into practical DfMA (Design for Manufacture and Assembly) solutions. Liaise directly with our procurement and factory teams in China to resolve technical discrepancies between design intent and manufacturing reality (tolerances, material selection, waterproofing details). Project Delivery: Act as the Owner’s Representative in technical matters. Make critical decisions on site issues and design changes swiftly to minimize cost and schedule impact. Thrive under pressure and manage multiple project timelines simultaneously. Requirements: Experience: Minimum 10-15 years of architectural experience, with at least 5 years in a managerial role leading a production team . Preferably delivered projects in Australia or New Zealand . Familiarity with local council approval processes is critical. Strong background in PPVC, MiC, or Modular Construction is highly preferred. Skills: Expertise in Revit, AutoCAD, and project management tools. Strong command of English (Written & Spoken) to manage AU/NZ consultants. Proficiency in Mandarin is essential to communicate technical details effectively with our supply chain and manufacturing partners in China. Attributes : High "Ownership" Mentality: You treat the project as your own. You do not clock off until the problem is solved. Resilient & Direct: You are not afraid to enforce standards and push the team to meet tight deadlines. Problem Solver: You provide solutions, not just identify problems. What We Offer: Competitive salary commensurate with experience. Opportunity to lead international projects in a cutting-edge construction sector. Key leadership position with direct reporting to the Director.

title: Architecture Permitting Coordinator
topic_label: singapore board architects, supervise architectural works, supervise architectural
description: Our Architecture team is expanding, we are seeking a proactive and independent Permitting Coordinator to join our dynamic team supporting major industrial and infrastructure projects, including waste treatment and environmental facilities. You will play a key role in managing permitting processes from start to finish, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements while maintaining strong relationships with authorities and stakeholders. This role is open for Singaporeans only given that it will involve authoriry clearance in most of our projects. Essential Duties: Coordinate and track all permitting activities across project phases. Liaise with government agencies (e.g., NEA, SCDF, BCA, JTC, PUB) to ensure timely approvals. Prepare, review, and submit documentation for regulatory compliance. Maintain accurate records of permit schedule, deadlines and resolve tight timelines without constant supervision Collaborate with consultants, contractors, and internal teams to resolve permitting issues. Proactively identify risks and propose mitigation strategies. Support audits and inspections related to permitting and compliance. Manages and keeps records of incoming & outgoing project submission and correspondences Ensures timely distribution of approved drawings and documents to clients, consultants & contractors in the respective project teams. Assists in the preparation of drawings (CAD, BIM etc.), project reports, presentation materials, printings of drawings/documents/reports for issuance. Ensures all documents submitted to clients/external parties are aligned with the project’s procedures. Prepares permitting minutes of meetings with clients, consultants and authority officers. Assists Lead Architects/Architect QPs in the coordination of internal and external meetings, setting up appointments/meeting invitations with authority officers. To be a successful candidate, you shall possess: Minimum Diploma in Architecture. 5-8 years of experience in permitting coordination preferably in industrial and infrastructure projects. Deep understanding of Singapore permitting process and well-versed with codes & regulations. Familiar with Singapore’s permitting regulatory landscape and key authority departments. Strong written and verbal skills with experience of liaising with consultants and authorities. Good interpersonal, organizational and facilitation skills to maintain good working relationships with key stakeholders. Documentation and File Management skills such as Project SharePoint, Microsoft Office, excel and Powerpoint Drafting skills such as CAD, Revit, Bentley, IFC-SG for Corenet X etc. Presentation skills such as SketchUp, Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign, PowerPoint etc. Familiar with ESPro, Corenet 2.0 and Corenet X platform for local authority submission. Values transparency, accountability and continuous learning opportunities. Please note: If you are being represented by a recruitment agency you will not be considered, to be considered you will need to apply directly to Jacobs. Jacobs complies with the Tripartite Guidelines on Fair Employment Practices (TGFEP), including the prevailing guidelines on recruitment. title: Research Fellow
topic_label: ai capabilities, ai, ai solutions
description: About SUTD SUTD is the world’s first Design AI university. With Design AI, artificial intelligence is treated as a partner and a member of the team – not just a tool. As a result of this unique SUTD treatment, AI and humans brainstorm, spar and prototype together, resulting in solutions that are elevated several-fold. This human-AI team concept has been made possible because of SUTD’s unique cohort-based interdisciplinary pedagogy. As a trailblazer in the field of design and technology education and research, SUTD has been pioneering innovative programmes and initiatives since its formation 16 years ago. As part its new mid-term growth strategy called SUTD Leap, it will work closely with industry to co-create solutions in both education and research – for a better world. About the Role We are in search of a highly motivated and accomplished Research Fellow to join our team and significantly contribute to our ongoing research project "Empowering Real-World 3D Scene Understanding: Navigating Noise, Distribution Shifts, and Incremental Learning". The successful candidate will collaborate closely with our research team, working on the development of ground-breaking algorithms and methodologies for Real-world 3D scene understanding problems. This role offers an exciting opportunity to engage in cutting-edge research and have a substantial influence on the field. What You'll Do Undertake research in the field of real-world 3D scene understanding. Design and execute experiments to validate the proposed approaches. Analyze and interpret data sourced from inputs, including images or point clouds. Stay up-to-date with the latest advancements in real-world 3D scene understanding and related fields, and integrate new findings into research projects. Prepare research papers for publication in prestigious conferences and journals. Deliver presentations on research findings at conferences and other pertinent events. Who We're Looking For PhD in computer science (artificial intelligence, machine learning, computer vision, or related fields), with a solid theoretical foundation and extensive research experience. Ability to handle high-intensity research work and demonstrate independent working capabilities. Significant research achievements in computer vision and machine learning, with strong publication records in 3D computer vision and top international journals or conferences. Strong programming skills, proficient in Python, C++, and familiar with common deep learning frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch. Team player with strong academic communication skills and collaborates effectively with team members from diverse backgrounds. International academic exchange and project management experience. Proficiency in both written and spoken English, with the ability to write academic papers and reports and communicate fluently with project collaborators. Strong self-motivation and problem-solving abilities, with capability to drive project progress within a team. Passionate and curiosity for research, willing to actively undertake research projects, mentor students, and participate in academic presentations and seminars.

description: About SUTD SUTD is the world’s first Design AI university. With Design AI, artificial intelligence is treated as a partner and a member of the team – not just a tool. As a result of this unique SUTD treatment, AI and humans brainstorm, spar and prototype together, resulting in solutions that are elevated several-fold. This human-AI team concept has been made possible because of SUTD’s unique cohort-based interdisciplinary pedagogy. As a trailblazer in the field of design and technology education and research, SUTD has been pioneering innovative programmes and initiatives since its formation 16 years ago. As part its new mid-term growth strategy called SUTD Leap, it will work closely with industry to co-create solutions in both education and research – for a better world. About The Job We are in search of a highly motivated and accomplished Research Assistant to join our team and significantly contribute to our ongoing research project “Agentic AI-RAN". The successful candidate will collaborate closely with our research team, working on the implementation of ground-breaking algorithms. This role offers an exciting opportunity to engage in cutting-edge research and have a substantial influence on the field. What You'll Do Undertake research on Agentic AI based AI-RAN. Develop Agentic AI algorithms for AI-RAN and under 5G NR standard. Work closely with Research Fellows. Stay up-to-date with the latest advancements in AI-RAN and Agentic AI. Prepare research papers for publication in prestigious conferences and journals. Deliver presentations on research findings at conferences and other pertinent events. Who We're Looking For A Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, or a related discipline. A strong background in communication theory and 3GPP standardization. Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, MATLAB, CUDA, and C++. Strong communication skills and the capability to work effectively in a collaborative research environment.

title: Field Ops Engineer
topic_label: industrial automation, automation engineering, automation systems
description: The successful candidate will be a part of the Business Development Team, reporting to the Business Development Manager. The Field Operations Engineer supports the expansion of VISCOY’s market space in Asset Inspection, Monitoring and Maintenance (AIMM) through field operations (proof-of-concepts (POC), product demonstrations and field deployments) and applications development for products in our portfolio. The Field Operations Engineer is the technical expert of our portfolio of Robotics and Digital Technologies. He/she is empowered to experiment and push the technical boundaries of the technologies that we represent in the field. Asset Inspection, Monitoring and Maintenance presents challenges that require the cross deployment of multiple technologies and customisation to the local context to overcome. The Field Operations Engineer leads the field deployment of our portfolio of technologies with our customers with the objective of overcoming these challenges through the development of more effective, cost efficient and safer solutions for the customer’s AIMM regime. The Field Operations Engineer works closely with our Technology Partners and Customers in the scoping, testing and deployment of our products and solutions. He/she will be the bridge between technology and real-world applications in the markets that we serve. To be successful in the role, the Field Operations Engineer is also required to have a good understanding of the limitations of our products. He/she works closely with the Repair and Maintenance Engineer in trouble-shooting, repair and developing the optimal operating parameters for the products in our portfolio. With deep understanding of technology gaps in AIMM, the Applications Development Engineer will be actively involved in scanning the market for relevant new technologies and developments. Technology is not bounded by geography or culture. The successful Field Operations Engineer enjoys the opportunity to travel and work in international teams with Technology Partners and Customers. The role will require the successful candidate to be proactive, possessing initiative, and a strong sense of ownership to take on and see-through projects assigned. He/she will need to collaborate closely both with internal and external stakeholders (technology partners and customers). Responsibilities Support the Business Development Team in: Development of use cases & applications for our Robotics and Digital Technology solutions in Asset Inspection, Monitoring & Maintenance Field Testing and Deployment of our portfolio of Robotics and Digital Technologies Solving AIMM challenges presented by our customers Be an expert of the Robotics and Digital Technologies in our portfolio To establish strong network, build close rapport and work effectively with both internal and external stakeholders. Manage and execute assigned projects to achieve agreed objectives Expand our technologies portfolio by keeping actively abreast of latest/emerging technologies that is relevant for the company’s current or adjacent market space. Develop market intelligence relevant for the development of Business Development strategies. Requirements An enthusiastic team player who can work independently Strong sense of ownership, positive mind-set and “can-do” attitude Highly motivated and results driven in the execution of assigned projects Enjoys travelling and working in international, cross-cultural teams Tertiary qualifications in Engineering/Technical disciplines. Fresh graduates are welcomed to apply. Candidates with experience will be preferred. Good spoken and written English Excellent communication and interpersonal skills Remuneration Attractive Basic Monthly Salary commensurate with relevant experience.

title: Senior AI UX / UI Designer

description: Senior AI UX / UI Designer As a Senior AI UX / UI Designer, you will work closely with Product Owners, engineers, and stakeholders to design, test, and refine AI-enabled user experiences. The role requires deep involvement across research, interaction design, usability testing, and delivery, with a strong emphasis on applying UX best practices to AI use cases. Key Responsibilities: AI-Centric UX & UI Design Design end-to-end user experiences for AI-enabled products, including conversational interfaces, agentic workflows, predictive and data-driven interactions. Translate AI capabilities, decision logic, and system outputs into clear, intuitive, and user-friendly interfaces. Create personas, journey maps, task flows, wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity UI designs. Design Systems & Documentation Create and maintain design systems, UI guidelines, and reusable components tailored for AI interfaces. Produce design documentation, annotated prototypes, and specifications to support development and adoption. Continuous Improvement Monitor post-launch user experience and identify opportunities to improve AI usability, explainability, and adoption. Stay current with emerging AI UX patterns, tools, and industry best practices. Requirements Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, AI/ML, or related field. 5–8 years of hands-on experience in UX/UI design and usability testing Demonstrated experience designing for AI-driven products (e.g. conversational AI, analytics-driven interfaces, automation, or decision-support systems) Strong experience in user research, journey mapping, and interaction design Experience designing conversational flows, multi-turn interactions, and AI error handling Proficiency with Figma , prototyping, wireframing, and usability testing tools Experience working in agile, cross-functional product teams 2 Positions Available Location : North East, Singapore Kindly indicate your current/last salary details and your notice period Interested candidates kindly forward your CV to michelleyy.dai@peoplesearch.jobs (Michelle Dai Yiying, Reg No: R1982577). title: Engineer II, Senior (Technology)
topic_label: ai capabilities, ai, ai solutions
description: The AI/Robotics Engineer will lead the lifecycle management, from development to production deployment of advanced AI and autonomous applications to address key challenges in the maritime and offshore industries. This role seamlessly integrates the development of sophisticated robotics perception models with theoperationalization of these solutions within an Azure Cloud environment using Agile methodologies. You will be part of the ABS Singapore Innovation and Research Center, working within cross-functional squads to deliver iterative value. You are expected to possess a blend of software engineering rigor, cloud architecture knowledge, and practical AI expertise to ensure our solutions are scalable, secure, and production ready. What You Will Do: Robotics Perception & Computer Vision Sensor Fusion & SLAM: Develop and implement robust sensor fusion algorithms, specifically combining Camera and LiDAR data to create accurate 3D representations and enable Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) in complex environments. Advanced Detection Capabilities : Lead the development of computer vision models for automated inspection, focusing on crack detection, pattern recognition, and anomaly detection on structural surfaces. Image Recognition : Engineer high-precision image recognition pipelines to identify key structural features and potential defects. Automation: Automate the interpretation of sensor data to reduce human intervention in inspection workflows. AI Model Development & Cloud Deployment Model Lifecycle Management : Develop, fine-tune, and integrate AI models for operational and safety-critical use cases, specifically targeting computer vision applications. Cloud Architecture : Architect, deploy, and manage scalable AI solutions on Microsoft Azure, utilizing services such as Azure Machine Learning, Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), and Azure Functions. Production Deployment : Transition prototype models into production-grade applications, optimizing code for latency, throughput, and cost-efficiency. MLOps Implementation : Design and maintain robust MLOps pipelines for model training, versioning,evaluation, and monitoring in production. Agile Development & Collaboration Agile Framework: Work within an Agile/Scrum framework, participating in sprint planning, daily     stand-ups, and retrospectives to ensure timely delivery of AI features. CI/CD Implementation : Adopt a DevOps mindset, ensuring continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD) practices are applied to machine learning and robotics workflows. Stakeholder Collaboration : Collaborate closely with Project Managers to translate business requirements into technical specifications and provide support to ensure smooth adoption of tools What You Will Need: Education and Experience Minimum Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Robotics, Engineering, or a related field. A Master’s or PhD is preferred. Minimum 2 years of experience in AI/ML development and deployment, preferably in a relevant field. Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities Robotics & Vision: Strong grasp of sensor fusion (Camera + LiDAR), SLAM, and experience in crack or pattern detection algorithms. Cloud Ecosystem: Hands-on experience with the Azure ecosystem (e.g., Azure MachineLearning, Blob Storage, Azure DevOps, Azure OpenAI Service). Framework Proficiency: Proficiency in machine learning frameworks such as TensorFlow or PyTorch. Scalable Deployment: Experience with distributed systems, cloud computing, and containerization tools (e.g., Kubernetes, Docker) for scalable AI deployment. API Integration: Proven experience in developing and fine-tuning AI models and integrating them via APIs. Soft Skills: Exceptional problem-solving skills, self-motivation, and strong communication skills to work effectively with non-technical stakeholders. description: ABOUT THE NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF EDUCATION (NIE) The National Institute of Education (NIE), Singapore , is Singapore’s national teacher education institute and we are proud to be an integral part of the nation’s education service. We play a key role in the preparation of teachers and in the provision of teacher professional and school leadership development programmes. We are committed to our vision of being An Institute of Distinction: Leading the Future of Education and our mission to Inspire Learning, Transform Teaching and Advance Research . Read more about NIE here . NIE invites applications for the position of part-time software developer for the project “PhET-style simulations to promote AI literacy”. The project’s goal is to develop a curated library of interactive, high-quality online resources for teachers that puts NIE at the forefront of AI literacy. To work towards this goal, the project will start out by developing customizable web-based simulations and corresponding avatar-based narrative instructional videos catering to three fundamental topics in AI literacy - Personalization using Machine Learning and Neural Networks , Ethical Issues in AIED , Human-AI Collaboration. These simulations would allow student teachers to experiment with relevant critical task features in a hands-on manner, thus improving their understanding of abstract technical concepts in AI and the implications of their usage in AI systems for education. Project evaluation would focus on usability evaluation and learning evaluation of the programmed simulations. Facilitation resources and validated design guidelines for developing effective AI literacy simulations will be made available to educational stakeholders upon project completion. This is a contract position till 30 November 2026. title: Founding Research Scientist

description:
About Us Ropedia is a Singapore-based, early-stage AI startup focused on building the data infrastructure for spatial and physical intelligence. Our core team brings together expertise from Tsinghua, Meta, Google, and UC Berkeley, and we have already achieved early-stage commercial viability and backed by top-tier VC and angel investors from Google, Nvidia, Amazon and etc. We develop proprietary wearable and mobile capture devices and models to collect, reconstruct, and perceive multimodal human Xperience in real-world scenarios. Our mission is to power the next generation of World Models and Embodied AI. We offer compensation competitive with top-tier tech giants, along with significant early-team equity and incentives. Role Overview Location: Singapore Type: Full-time Job Description You will join the founding research team
and lead model design and research in core areas such as world models, 3D/4D
perception, and egocentric understanding. Multiple research tracks are open
under this role, and candidates may match one or more directions based on their
background. Responsibilities ·     Design and implement models for world and scene understanding using egocentric, multi-view, and multimodal data. ·     Work closely with engineering and hardware teams to deliver reusable, maintainable research code and internal tools. ·     Co-design data collection protocols, evaluation metrics, and ablation studies. ·     Produce research papers, internal technical reports, and demo systems at appropriate stages of the project. Requirements ·     PhD (or equivalent research experience) in Computer Vision, Machine Learning, Graphics, Robotics, or related fields. ·     Strong track record in at least one of the following areas: human modeling, 3D/4D reconstruction, egocentric vision, or scene/world modeling. ·     Proficient in Python and mainstream deep learning frameworks (PyTorch preferred). ·     Solid foundation in 3D geometry, camera models, and numerical optimization. ·     Able to drive ideas from concept to implementation and deployment. Clear communication skills are required. Research Directions A. Human Modeling & Motion Understanding Focus: Human/hand/face modeling, motion and deformation priors, human–object interaction, and affordance modeling. Preferred Background: Experience in human pose/shape estimation, SMPL-type models, motion capture, or motion generation. B. 3D/4D Reconstruction & Neural Rendering Focus: Multi-view and dynamic scene reconstruction, NeRF/Gaussian Splatting, novel view synthesis. Preferred Background: Experience in 3D reconstruction or neural
rendering; familiarity with camera calibration and bundle adjustment (BA). C. Egocentric Vision & Interaction Focus: Egocentric action and intention understanding, hand–object interaction, gaze/attention modeling, and task structure modeling. Preferred Background: Experience in video understanding, action recognition, or egocentric vision. D. Scene Reconstruction & World Modeling Focus: Long-term consistent 3D/4D scene mapping, scene graphs, object- and space-centric representations, and spatial reasoning. Preferred Background: Experience in large-scale mapping, semantic reconstruction, or agent world models.

title: AI Engineer

description: JOB OVERVIEW: As an AI Engineer, you will leverage cutting-edge AI to solve complex, industry-specific problems, particularly within the maritime sector. You will be instrumental in a rapidly evolving, client-centric organization, making a significant impact by thinking critically, articulating a compelling vision, and guiding both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Your role requires a proven ability to architect complex AI/ML solutions from problem framing to production-ready design, a profound understanding of diverse business models, and strong consultative sales skills to identify strategic pain points solvable by AI. You will build functional AI/ML prototypes with clean, modular code for production, assess data readiness, identify data gaps, and recommend robust data governance frameworks. Additionally, you will identify emerging trends, synthesize complex information, and present strategic recommendations to senior leadership and external audiences. Success hinges on your ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear business value, build strong client relationships, and drive consensus across diverse teams. You must be highly motivated to drive initiatives independently in a dynamic startup environment, with a passion for continuous learning and innovation. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: •	Proactively identify and engage with high-value business opportunities and potential clients who can significantly benefit from advanced AI and machine learning solutions. Deeply understand client business challenges, pain points, and strategic goals, translating them into clear, solvable AI/ML problems. •	Design end-to-end AI solutions, articulating compelling value propositions and the strategic roadmap for adoption. •	Conduct in-depth assessments of client data infrastructure, data governance, data quality, and overall data maturity. •	Make expert judgment calls on data "AI-readiness" and provide actionable recommendations for data improvement, gap identification, and strategic data capitalization opportunities. •	Rapidly develop functional AI prototypes and Proof-of-Concepts (POCs) that are architecturally sound, adhere to best practices for maintainability, and are designed for production. •	Continuously research and evaluate the latest AI advancements, including Generative AI, Large Language Models (LLMs), Deep Learning, and MLOps methodologies. Synthesize findings into actionable insights, influencing our company's strategic roadmap, service offering development, and contributing to industry thought leadership through presentations to leadership and external forums (moderate to high emphasis). •	Collaborate effectively with the internal AI Production team, ensuring prototypes and solution designs are well-documented, understood, and seamlessly integrated into the full development lifecycle. •	Clearly define the business case, expected return on investment (ROI), and measurable benefits of proposed AI/ML implementations, simplifying complex technical concepts for non-technical audiences. QUALIFICATIONS AND EXPERIENCES: •	Minimum Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related quantitative field. Master's or Ph.D. preferred. •	At least 5+ years of progressive experience in AI/ML solution design, data science, or AI engineering, with a significant portion in a client-facing or consulting capacity. •	Proven experience in designing, developing, and deploying functional AI/ML prototypes that demonstrate business value and are engineered for production. •	Expert proficiency in Python and extensive experience with core AI/ML libraries (e.g., scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch). •	Demonstrated hands-on experience with Generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs), including frameworks like LangChain, for designing and prototyping novel applications. •	Strong practical experience with Google Cloud Platform (GCP) services relevant to AI/ML (e.g., Vertex AI, BigQuery, Cloud Storage, Dataflow, Cloud Functions). •	Proficiency with Docker for containerization and a foundational understanding of container orchestration (e.g., Kubernetes concepts for GKE). •	Solid understanding of MLOps principles for reproducibility, experiment tracking (e.g., MLfl ow, Vertex AI ML Metadata/Model Registry), and model versioning. •	High-level expertise in data governance frameworks, data quality assessment (including tools/libraries like Great Expectations), metadata management (e.g., Google Cloud Data Catalog), and data architecture principles. •	Strong expertise in distributed computing and parallel processing techniques is vital for handling large-scale AI workloads. •	A proven track record of innovations and executions in deep learning, demonstrated through shipping products or first-author publications at leading AI conferences, is a strong differentiator. •	Experience in the container shipping industry or related logistics/supply chain domains is highly advantageous. •	Excellent presentation, documentation, and stakeholder management skills. Working hours: Mon to Fri, 8:30am to 5:30pm

title: Senior Data Scientist
topic_label: data engineer, data engineers, data analyst
description: Synthesis x Senior Data Scientist At Synthesis, we push the boundaries of what’s possible with Open Data. It’s a fast-paced, rapidly-changing environment at the intersection of data science, cultural analysis & brand consultancy. We call this Open Source Intelligence for brands. As a Senior Data Scientist at Synthesis, you will play a key role in shaping our data science practice and driving innovation across both client projects and internal products. You will lead end-to-end data initiatives, delivering project work from shaping datasets and building models to designing scalable data workflows that power the insights and decision frameworks we deliver. You will work with diverse open data sources, ranging from search, social, and e-commerce, to academic papers and patents, to understand audience behaviour and anticipate how needs will evolve over time. In this role, you will not only contribute hands-on but also guide junior data scientists, act as a thought partner to cultural strategists, and help define the roadmap for our data products and infrastructure. We are a small, collaborative team with diverse backgrounds, spanning computer science and engineering, anthropology, and law. We value curiosity, creativity, and a spirit of experimentation. If you are excited about combining data and culture to solve real-world problems, we would love to hear from you! What you’ll need. 5+ years of experience in data science, with a strong record of delivering impactful projects from data exploration through to production deployment. Deep proficiency in Python and SQL, with strong software engineering fundamentals (clean, modular, well-documented code, testing, and version control with Git). Proven experience designing and building scalable data workflows or data-driven products, including architecting pipelines, modular components, and reusable frameworks. Experience applying large language models or advanced NLP techniques (e.g. embeddings, semantic parsing, topic modelling, text classification); familiarity with processing Asian languages is a plus. Strong grasp of statistical methods and core machine learning algorithms, with practical experience using libraries such as scikit-learn, statsmodels, and the ability to select appropriate models based on data characteristics and problem context. Comfortable working with large, messy, and unstructured datasets, particularly text and image-based data. Ability to translate complex technical insights into clear, actionable recommendations for non-technical stakeholders, and collaborate closely with strategists to drive business outcomes. A collaborative mindset, with openness to feedback and diverse perspectives. Bonus points if you have the following. Experience leading or mentoring junior data scientists, including setting technical direction and supporting professional growth. Experience building and maintaining production-grade data pipelines (e.g. Airflow, Dagster) or integrating data from APIs, web scraping, or third-party providers. Familiarity with cloud environments such as Google Cloud or AWS, and deploying data science workflows at scale Why Synthesis? Synthesis is known for delivering exceptional data models and products that uncover the stories of the people they represent and inspire our partners to act with confidence. Partnering with the world’s most successful brands in fashion, beauty, content, and travel, we’re building specialised solutions to solve problems of planning for the future. Rooted in culture, tested in data science, we spot and anticipate changes and connections in culture to inspire action and help brands grow with a new wave of audiences. After three years of iterative development and refinement, we are preparing to launch a series of products over the next 5 years. We will be at the forefront of a shift towards leveraging open data to develop rich, honest, human insight. In an industry that has, for too long, relied on a ‘question-response’ approach to understanding changes in behaviour, we prioritise layering behavioural, performative, search, and sales data to highlight discrepancies between what people say they do and what they actually do. We see a $40 billion industry that has failed to innovate for too long, and we are just getting started. About Synthesis We do Human Centred Data Science. It’s a way of reimaging open data sources from a human perspective. It prioritises behaviour and context to unravel why people do what they do, at scale.  By blending data science with human and market intelligence, we help our clients spot early signals of change and predict implications for business. Diverse by design. Synthesis is a team of digital researchers, gaming designers, data forecasters, network scientists, ethnographers, and engineers.  Cultural and category experts train our models – ensuring they detect measures that matter most – whilst data scientists uncover patterns the eye cannot see. Building bespoke, comprehensive datasets where none exist. We work with open data - search, ratings, social, and sales.  By layering many signals, we build robust and relevant data-driven stories around brands, businesses, and consumer behaviour. Asia first, global applied. Our products must work in Asia first - the hardest test markets.  We continue to make significant investments in language and capabilities across Asian-specific platforms.  Now applied in Europe, LatAm, and the USA.

title: AI SOftware Engineer

description:

We are currently looking to hire a AI Software Engineer . This is an exciting opportunity to expand your skill set, achieve job satisfaction and work-life balance. More details as below. JOB TYPE: Long Term Contract Job Scope: Design, build, and operate
production-grade agentic and GenAI systems—end to end. You’ll ship services
(not just notebooks): robust APIs, reusable components, and secure pipelines
that connect LLMs, tools, knowledge, and enterprise systems. You’ll pair strong
software engineering with modern AI practices (RAG, agent orchestration, policy
chains, evals) to deliver measurable business outcomes at scale. 1. Agent & Application
Engineering Design
     multi-agent systems (MAS) with planning, tool-use, and delegation (e.g.,
     LangGraph/Semantic Kernel); expose them via REST/gRPC APIs
     (FastAPI/Express/Java/Go). Implement
     tool adapters (SQL, search, document stores, web calls, code exec) with
     strict type contracts and safe sandboxes. Build
     model gateway integrations (OpenAI/Azure OpenAI/Bedrock/Vertex;
     self-hosted vLLM/TGI) with routing, rate-limits, retries, and fallback
     chains. 2. Retrieval, Data &
Knowledge Stand
     up RAG services: chunking, enrichment, embeddings, indexing, hybrid/vector
     search (pgvector/Pinecone/Weaviate; OpenSearch/Azure AI Search). Implement
     ingestion pipelines (Airflow/Prefect/Celery/Ray) for docs, tickets, chat,
     and ERP/CRM data; handle PII redaction and metadata governance. Optimize
     retrieval quality (chunking strategies, re-rankers, query rewriting) with
     offline/online evaluation and A/B tests. 3. Quality, Testing &
Evaluation Treat
     prompts and graphs as code: version, diff, and test them (unit tests for
     prompts/tools; golden sets; regression suites). Build
     evaluation harnesses (latency, cost, accuracy, toxicity, hallucination,
     guardrail hit-rates); wire into CI. Add
     drift detection for conversational systems; implement safe shutdown and
     auto-rollback. 4. Platform &
Operations Package
     services as containers; deploy to Kubernetes with Helm/Argo CD; configure
     autoscaling, HPA/VPA, and resource quotas. Implement
     policy chains and guardrails (OPA/Gatekeeper for policy, Presidio for PII,
     Trivy for image scanning). Instrument
     deep observability: tracing (OpenTelemetry), metrics (Prometheus), logs
     (ELK/OPENSEARCH), cost meters per request/model. 5. Security &
Compliance Manage
     secrets (HashiCorp Vault/KMS), signed images, SBOMs; enforce
     least-privilege IAM. Build
     tenant isolation and data residency controls; implement red/blue team
     prompts and jailbreak defenses. 6. Integration &
Enterprise Workflows Ship
     connectors and events for SAP/CRM/ITSM and Kafka topics; design
     idempotent, retry-safe processors. Automate
     business workflows with pro-code services first; expose low-code surfaces
     only where appropriate. 7. Collaboration &
Leadership Partner
     with Product, Data, and Platform teams to define SLAs/SLOs and success
     metrics. Mentor
     engineers on “AI as software” practices; run design reviews and
     postmortems. Qualifications The ideal candidate should
possess: 1+ years in software engineering (prod services, not just prototypes),
     including 1+ year leading small projects. Strong in one systems language (Python/TypeScript/Go/Java) and comfortable in a second. Hands-on with containers, Kubernetes, CI/CD (GitHub Actions/GitLab/Jenkins), IaC (Terraform), and cloud (Azure/AWS/GCP). PracticalLLM experience: building RAG/agent apps, prompt design, tool-use, and safety patterns. Data
     skills: designing schemas, batch/stream pipelines, and search indexes;
     proficiency with SQL and one vector DB. Testing
     mindset: unit/integration tests, load tests, golden datasets for LLM
     evals. Security
     basics: secrets, policies, scanning, and least-privilege IAM. Preferred Qualifications Agent
     orchestration (LangGraph, Semantic Kernel) and distributed compute (Ray)
     in production. Search/retrieval
     tuning (BM25 + vector hybrid, re-ranking, query planning). Observability
     at scale with OpenTelemetry; cost/perf optimization across model/router
     layers. Experience
     in regulated or high-throughput domains (e.g., telco, finance,
     healthcare); multi-tenant and data-residency patterns. Domain
     integrations (SAP/CRM/ITSM), event-driven architectures (Kafka/Debezium),
     and policy enforcement (OPA/Gatekeeper). Familiarity
     with TM Forum APIs / BSS-OSS patterns is a plus (if in telco context). Tech Stack (Illustrative) Languages:
     Python, TypeScript/Node.js (plus Go/Java bonus) Frameworks:
     FastAPI/Express, LangGraph/Semantic Kernel, Ray/Celery, Airflow/Prefect Storage/Search:
     Postgres, Redis, S3/Blob; pgvector/Pinecone/Weaviate; OpenSearch/Azure AI
     Search LLM
     Runtime: OpenAI/Azure OpenAI/Bedrock/Vertex; vLLM/TGI; inference
     routers/gateways Platform:
     Docker, Kubernetes, Helm, Argo CD, Terraform, Vault, Istio Observability:
     OpenTelemetry, Prometheus/Grafana, ELK/OpenSearch. To submit your application please email your CV to Khalid Farooq at khalid@aven-sys.com. description: Senior Data Consultant ABOUT US Syniti , part of Capgemini, tackles the hardest work in data for the world’s largest organizations. Syniti’s Data First strategy transforms data from an afterthought into a strategic asset—unlocking insights, reducing risk, and fueling growth. With over 5,000 successful projects, we support the full data lifecycle through a unified platform for migration, quality, replication, matching, master data management, analytics, governance, and strategy. Operating globally across industries, Syniti’s award-winning software, expert consultants give enterprises a unique edge in managing and maximizing their data. THE ROLE We are recruiting talented and motivated individuals to join our team delivering data projects for clients across the globe. As a Senior Data Consultant at Syniti, you will work closely with functional consultants and key customer resources, gathering requirements, building conversion objects, and driving independent value through all data project phases. You will comfortably use your data skills and the Syniti data management platform to assess and iterate through design, build and deployment, ensuring that the highest customer value is derived from your assigned objects. As a member of a Syniti Data Migration team, you will execute against the best practices for successful business transformation and become skilled in the strongest platform to meet all of our customer’s needs. WHAT YOU WILL DO After an initial training period, a Senior Consultant will be expected to: • Apply Syniti’s data migration methodology and development standards across projects. • Develop and maintain data migration rules, transformation logic, reports, and data load programs using Syniti tools. • Execute end-to-end data loads, reconciliations, and cutover activities for assigned functional areas. • Analyze and validate source and target data structures, supporting data cleansing and issue resolution with business data owners. • Contribute to data migration design through data analysis, reporting, and collaboration with Syniti and client teams. • Participate in Business Blueprint workshops and facilitate data mapping sessions. • Maintain clear documentation and communicate progress, risks, and status to Syniti leadership and customer PMO. • Act as a functional/technical SME, collaborating with other process teams to identify and resolve cross-team data issues. WHAT IT TAKES • Degree level qualification in Computer Science (or other technology-related field) • Experience in ETL / Data Migration • Functional and technical ERP/SAP experience • Project experience across multiple ERP implementations • Database experience - SQL Server, Oracle etc. • Customer-facing skills • Excellent written and verbal communication skills • Willingness to travel according to project requirements up to 100% • Syniti ADM experience would be an advantage Be fully aligned with our core values: Think Big - Be courageous and bold. Aspire to greatness. Relentlessly pursue market innovation. Set the standard by which others follow. Create solutions that have a meaningful impact. Solve the challenges our customers don’t know they have. Be Curious - Be a lifelong learner. Seek out new ideas to serve customers. Understand our competition and the world. Be permanently dissatisfied with the status quo. Challenge preconceptions. Focus on the future rather than yesterday. Take Action - Be the first. Don’t wait. Take accountability. Inspire others by doing. Fail fast and learn from mistakes. Make a difference every day. Stronger Together - Respect, trust and look out for each other. Celebrate diverse perspectives. Listen. Build connections and belonging. Act with integrity. Give back. One Syniti family. WHAT WE OFFER Trust that you are proficient at what you do. At Syniti, you will find a supportive environment and access to learning tools, but micromanagement is not our thing. Growth. We are growing rapidly and steadily solving the biggest challenges enterprise companies are faced with today. There was never a better time to join and grow with us. Most importantly, you will have the chance to shape our journey and share in our success story. Support. We all rely on each other and enable each other to be successful. You won’t stand alone. Curiosity and genuine interest in you. We all have our different stories, all equally fascinating, with each depicting a different journey, and we want to hear them all. Recognition. We are the sum of individual achievements, and we always take the time to celebrate them. An open organisation. Hierarchies are not our thing, and access is something we make sure of across the board. We are a family where everyone is just as important, everyone’s work is seen, and ideas are valued. OUR COMMITMENT TO INCLUSION At Syniti, we’re committed to creating a respectful, inclusive, and fair workplace where everyone belongs and thrives. We believe that diverse perspectives make us stronger — and we value the unique backgrounds, experiences, and voices each person brings to our team. We welcome applicants based on their skills and potential, and we’re dedicated to ensuring equal opportunities for all, regardless of personal background. description: About the Role We are seeking an experienced Business Analyst (Data Policy) to support a Singapore Government Agency in developing, implementing, and maintaining robust data governance and data protection policies. description: Product area Google Play offers music, movies, books, apps and games for devices, powered by the cloud. It syncs across devices and on the web. They make it possible for people to do things like buy an ebook or song on their Android phone, then have it instantly available on their laptop. The Google Play team enhances the Android ecosystem by giving developers and partners a premium store where they can reach millions of users. Job description Google's leadership team hand-picks thorny business challenges, and members of BizOps work in small teams to find solutions. As part of this team you fully immerse yourself in data collection, draw insight from analysis, and then zoom out to develop compelling, synthesized recommendations. Taking strategy one step further, you also persuasively communicate your recommendations to senior-level executives, roll-up your sleeves to help drive implementation and check back-in to see the impact of your recommendations. This role offers a unique opportunity to work closely with mobile games and apps developers, translating data into AI-driven products that directly enable the growth of their businesses. Qualifications Job responsibilities Design and implement data science pipelines, utilizing techniques such as Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and Fine-tuning, to build advanced Generative AI solutions. Preferred qualifications Experience in Generative AI evaluation methodologies and metrics (e.g., LLM-as-a-judge, safety, quality, and bias detection). Experience applying Generative AI technologies to enterprise-scale products and solutions, particularly within a quantitative domain. Understanding of MLOps/LLMOps practices for productionizing AI agents and models. Proficiency with cloud-native platforms (e.g., Google Cloud/GCP). Ability to break down ambiguous problems and propose impactful solutions through data modeling and system design. Excellent attention to detail and effective verbal/written communication skills, including the ability to communicate technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.

description: At ResMed, we’re on a mission to change lives with every breath — and our global supply chain is rapidly transforming to enable growth, resilience, quality, and value at scale. We’re hiring a Director of Automation & Systems Integration to lead the design, standardisation, and deployment of automation and digital-integration platforms across our manufacturing and distribution network. This role builds the automation backbone of our next-generation supply chain; engineering scalable, reliable, and intelligent systems that increase productivity, improve asset performance, and enable scale. The Opportunity As Director of Automation & Systems Integration leads the global engineering organization responsible for robotics, automation, controls, and systems integration across all supply chain sites. You will define automation design principles and standard platforms, and form strategic engineering partnerships with technology providers and Automation OEMS — going well beyond a “smart customer” to co-create, refine, and scale automation solutions across the network. This role integrates automation with MES, WES/WCS, SCADA, PLC/controls, and digital data layers to enable real-time visibility, predictive insights, and improved decision making. You’ll work closely with Industrial Engineering, Manufacturing Engineering, Global Logistics & Distribution, Planning, Quality, Digital Supply Chain, and Product Development to ensure automation solutions deliver throughput, reliability, safety, and long-term capacity. This role is foundational to our Network 2030+ vision: a globally consistent, modular, automation-ready network capable of scaling with speed, resilience, and confidence. Key Responsibilities Automation Strategy & Network 2030+ Alignment Build and execute a global automation strategy that accelerates automation as the core productivity driver of our next-generation supply chain. Define standardised automation design principles, modular architectures, controls frameworks, and platform strategies deployable across regions. Develop a multi-year roadmap for robotics, AGVs/AMRs, AS/RS, automated test platforms, and material movement automation aligned to network growth and supply chain transformation. Serve as a strategic partner in evaluating automation opportunities and ensuring investments align with business value, operational needs, and supply chain transformation priorities. Systems Integration & the Digital Thread Lead systems integration of automation assets with MES, WES/WCS, SCADA, PLC/Controls IoT, and enterprise data systems Establish integration toolkits, templates, interface libraries, and testing protocols to ensure consistency and reliability. Develop and partner to create standard integration architectures, integration toolkits, and interface libraries that ensure interoperability, scalability, and reliability. Partner with Digital Supply Chain teams to connect automation performance data to real-time dashboards, predictive analytics and digital twin, enabling smarter CapEx and planning decisions . Ensure new and existing automation platforms are integrated securely and consistently, enabling faster deployment and reduced engineering rework. Robotics, Automation Platforms & Deployment Excellence Lead engineering evaluation, technology selection, and deployment of robotics, automation technologies across manufacturing and distribution environments (paperless systems, robotics, LSR automation, AGVs/AMRs, material handling systems, automated test and inspection). Govern engineering specifications, vendor selection, lifecycle cost models, standard controls architecture, FAT/SAT protocols, and commissioning excellence. Establish an Automation Center of Excellence to accelerate standardisation, replicate best practice, and scale solutions globally at pace. Champion design-for-replication to enable rapid, reliable deployment of automation platforms across sites. Asset Performance, Reliability & Automation Health Ensure all automation and integrated systems produce accurate, high resolution performance data to support OEE improvement, predictive maintenance, and capacity optimization. Partner with Industrial Engineering and Asset Performance teams to leverage downtime analytics, bottleneck analysis, TPB aligned reliability engineering practices. Embed automation health monitoring, diagnostics, and predictive analytics to improve uptime, throughput, and line stability. Enable real-time and global automation dashboards, linking physical automation performance directly into planning and continuous improvement. New Site Establishment & Automation-Ready Design Define automation and integration requirements for new supply chain sites, ensuring automation readiness from concept to commissioning. Partner with Facility and Industrial Engineering to embed automation early in layout design, utilities planning, safety architecture, and CVS (Commissioning, Validation, Scale) processes. Deploy modular automation solutions that reduce commissioning times, improve CapEx efficiency,  and enable predictable scalability across the network . Leadership, Governance & Capability Building Build a world-class global team of automation, robotics, controls, and systems integration engineers. Establish engineering governance to ensure global standardization, interoperability, and consistent decision-making across all sites. Create training pathways and capability-building programs to develop future-ready, digitally enabled automation talent. Foster a culture grounded in engineering rigor, innovation, collaboration, and continuous improvement. About You 12–15+ years in automation engineering, robotics, systems integration, or advanced manufacturing engineering, including 5+ years in senior leadership roles. Deep expertise in automation platforms including robotics, AGVs/AMRs, automated test systems, material storage and handling automation, and controls architecture and digital integration (MES, SCADA, PLC, WES/WCS, IoT). Strong manufacturing automation experience is essential; exposure to distribution automation is valuable. Experience with TPM, reliability engineering, or maintenance strategies for automated systems is highly beneficial Demonstrated success delivering global automation deployments in highly regulated, multi-site supply chain environments. Strong understanding of OEE, bottleneck analysis, predictive maintenance, flow modeling, and asset performance improvement. Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Automation, Mechatronics, Systems, Electrical, or Manufacturing Engineering; advanced digital or robotics certifications a plus. Exceptional leadership and communication skills, with the ability to influence senior stakeholders across global supply chain landscapes. Success Metrics Automation Excellence Deliver measurable productivity and flow improvements through scalable automation solutions. Platform Standardization: Drive adoption of standardized automation architectures across the majority of applicable sites within the first 18–24 months. Asset Performance & Reliability Improve uptime and automation reliability through enhanced diagnostics, predictive maintenance, and data-driven monitoring Digital Thread & Integration Velocity Reduce integration time and engineering rework by implementing standard architectures and reusable components. Speed, Scalability & Capital Efficiency Shorten automation deployment timelines at new or expanding sites through modular design and engineering governance. People & Capability Build and sustain a high-performing automation engineering team with strong engagement and development progression. Why Join ResMed This is a rare opportunity to define the automation and integration blueprint of a global, next-generation supply chain. You’ll architect intelligent, connected automation systems that power how we grow, scale, and deliver — enabling extraordinary outcomes for patients worldwide. At ResMed, you’ll join a global team committed to excellence, innovation, and purpose. Together, we’ll build the most integrated, data-driven, and scalable supply chain engineering network in the world.

title: Robotics Engineer (Humanoid Robots)

description: What you’ll do · Build and maintain robotic software for humanoid robots using ROS/ROS 2 (C++/Python) on Ubuntu OS. You’ll work across perception, localization, navigation, manipulation, and HRI. · Integrate sensors (LiDAR, depth/RGB cameras, IMU), develop SLAM/localization, and tune planners (Nav2, SLAM Toolbox, tf2, ros2_control). · Prototype, simulate, and validate behaviors in Gazebo/Isaac Sim, then harden for field deployment at customer sites (industrial/manufacturing, logistic, transport, etc.). · Develop demos and production apps; leverage ROS/Android-based interfaces and open development hooks for humanoid robots where applicable. · Own the full cycle: requirements → prototyping → on-robot testing → CI/integration → rollout → monitoring & performance tuning. · Collaborate with Robotics Solutions & Delivery team to scope use-cases, conduct site acceptance tests, and document best practices and runbooks. · Assist in risk assessments for General Safety requirements for industrial mobile robots with actively controlled stability (legged, wheeled, or other forms of locomotion). What you’ll bring · 2~5+ years in robotics (or exceptional projects/internships) with ROS/ROS 2, C++/Python, Git, and Linux-based OS (e.g. Ubuntu). · Hands-on in one or more: motion planning & control (kinematics/dynamics, trajectory generation), navigation (Nav2), perception (OpenCV/PCL), or state estimation. · Experience integrating and calibrating typical robot sensors (LiDAR, depth/RGB cameras, IMU); comfort with drivers, transforms, and time sync. · Fluency with simulation and on-robot debugging; familiarity with CI/CD and containerization is a plus. · Strong problem-solving and field-readiness (you enjoy getting robots working in the wild, not just in the lab). · Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Robotics, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related field. Nice-to-haves · Humanoid locomotion / whole-body control / MPC / loco-manipulation; experience with biped balance & gait control. · Android app development or experience with vendor SDKs for robot apps (e.g. UBTECH or similar). · Knowledge of functional safety. Why Stellaris Robotics Build Singapore’s next wave of humanoid robot applications with the exclusive UBTECH distributor. You’ll work directly on platforms like Walker Tienkung, Walker S2 (autonomous battery swap, ROS/Android dev environment) and help bring real deployments to life. How we’ll evaluate · Portfolio/GitHub or brief write-ups of ROS/ROS 2 projects (what you built, stack used, performance achieved). · Practical exercise: bring up a perception/nav stack on a provided dataset or robot, then present tuning decisions and safety considerations. Apply Send your CV and links (GitHub, videos, papers) to lorenaluo@stellarisrobot.com with subject “Robotics Engineer – [Your Name]”. Include 3–5 bullets on your most relevant ROS/ROS 2 work.
